sections.paragraph:
comp. pr.n. אָשֵׁר).

(1) to be straight, right, i.q. יָשַׁר especially used of a straight way, hence also of what is upright, erect, whence comes the signification of firmness and strength, in the Talmud.

(2) to go straight on, and generally to go, Pro 9:6.

(3) to be successful, to prosper, to be fortunate, compare the kindred roots יָשַׁר No. 3, כָּשֵׁר and עָשַׁר.

Piel אִשֵּׁר-

(1) to guide, or lead straight, Pro 23:19 Isa 1:17, אַשְּׁרוּ חָמֹוץ “lead the oppressor right,” into the right way, (unless, comparing Pual No. 2, we render with the ancient versions, ῥύσασθε ἀδικούμενον. Vulg. subvenite oppresso, pr. make the oppressed happy), and generally to lead, Isa 3:12 9:15 .

(2) intrans. to go on, Pro 4:14.

(3) to pronounce happy, or fortunate, Gen 30:13 Ps: 72:17 Pro 31:28 Son 6:9 Job 29:11.

Pual אֻשַּׁר and אוּשַּׁר-

(1) to be led, Isa 9:15.-(2) to be made fortunate, Psa 41:3 Pro 3:18.

Derivative nouns are אָשֵׁר, אֲשֵׁרָה, אֶשֶׁר, אשֶׁר, אֲשׁוּר, אַשּׁוּר, תְּאַשּׁוּר, אֻשַּׁרְנָא.

אשר

(“fortunate,” “happy,” compare Gen 20:13), [Asher], pr.n.-

(1) of a son of Jacob and his concubine Zilpah ( Gen 30:13 35:26 ), ancestor of the tribe of the same name ( Num 1:40, 41 ), whose boundaries are described as on the northern border of the holy land, -Josh. 19:2431 . The Gentile noun is אֲשֵׁרִי Jud. 1:32 .-(2) a town to the east of Shechem, Jos 17:17.
